text.title: Rust Backend Project Structure and Axum Router
text.summary: Directory layout for Axum backends, AppState wiring, and router composition by feature module.

section.title: Directory Layout

rule.statement: Organise the crate as routes/ → handlers/ → services/ → db/; handlers stay HTTP-only, all business logic lives in services.
rule.rationale: Thin handlers are testable with axum::test without standing up a real HTTP server. Services are testable with plain async functions and mocked DB traits.

section.p: Standard layout: src/main.rs (entry), src/lib.rs (library root), src/config.rs, src/error.rs, src/routes/, src/handlers/, src/services/, src/models/, src/db/, src/middleware/. Also: migrations/, tests/.

rule.statement: AppState must be Clone (cheap — use Arc inside); pass it via with_state() so every handler gets typed access without globals.
rule.rationale: Global mutable state defeats Rust's ownership guarantees and makes parallel tests fragile. AppState as a Clone+Send+Sync value composes safely with Tower middleware.

rule.statement: Load configuration from environment variables via a Config struct at startup; never use dotenv in production binaries.
rule.rationale: dotenv silently overwrites environment variables set by the deployment system. In production, env vars come from the orchestrator (Docker, systemd, k8s); dotenv is for local dev only.
